items.question: DBSCAN is best described as:
options: Density-based clustering using eps and min_samples with explicit noise labels
Partitioning into k groups by minimizing centroid variance
Supervised classification by majority vote among neighbors
Hierarchical agglomeration with a fixed linkage threshold only
items.answer: Density-based clustering using eps and min_samples with explicit noise labels
items.explanation: DBSCAN chains dense eps-neighborhoods from core points and marks sparse points as noise — it does not take k or use centroids like k-means.

items.question: A point is a core point when:
options: Its eps-neighborhood contains at least min_samples points (including itself)
It is closest to the global centroid
It has the smallest k-distance in the dataset
It belongs to the largest cluster after k-means init
items.answer: Its eps-neighborhood contains at least min_samples points (including itself)
items.explanation: Core status depends only on neighbor count within eps versus min_samples — the canonical definition in this topic.

items.question: In scikit-learn DBSCAN output, label −1 indicates:
options: A noise point not assigned to any cluster
The largest cluster ID
A border point that seeds expansion
Missing data imputed as outlier
items.answer: A noise point not assigned to any cluster
items.explanation: Noise points are neither core nor border — sparse regions DBSCAN deliberately leaves unclustered.

items.question: Increasing eps while holding min_samples fixed generally:
options: Merges neighborhoods and tends to reduce cluster count and noise fraction
Guarantees more clusters of equal size
Has no effect because DBSCAN uses k not eps
Forces every point to become border only
items.answer: Merges neighborhoods and tends to reduce cluster count and noise fraction
items.explanation: Larger eps connects more points within one neighborhood, creating longer density chains and fewer separate components.

items.question: Why scale features before DBSCAN?
options: So eps applies comparably across dimensions in distance calculations
Because DBSCAN requires binary inputs only
To convert unsupervised learning to supervised
Because min_samples must equal the number of features
items.answer: So eps applies comparably across dimensions in distance calculations
items.explanation: Unscaled high-magnitude features dominate distance; eps-neighbor counts then reflect units, not meaningful similarity — see Distance Metrics.

items.question: DBSCAN vs k-means: which statement is correct?
options: DBSCAN can find non-convex clusters and label outliers; k-means assigns every point to one of k centroids
Both require the same hyperparameter k
k-means always produces noise label −1 for outliers
DBSCAN minimizes within-cluster sum of squares to centroids
items.answer: DBSCAN can find non-convex clusters and label outliers; k-means assigns every point to one of k centroids
items.explanation: Different objectives and outputs — density connectivity vs centroid partitioning; detailed k-means mechanics live in the k-Means topic.
